channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Maldives cave dive deaths leave us with many theories and one big question
item.description: The question of why experienced divers, including a professional instructor and a marine ecology professor with thousands of dives under her belt, would descend to depths of more than 50 metres using recreational gear — let alone enter a cave while doing so — baffles members of the relatively close-knit deep-sea diving community.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Thorpe

media:group.media:description: A deep-sea rescue diver, trailing a guide line, explores the inner sections of a cave in the Maldives where five Italians died.

item.title: Iran submits revised proposal, Trump says ceasefire 'a lot closer'
item.description: Iran and Pakistan submit a revised proposal ‌to the United States ‌to end the war and ‌reopen the Strait ‌of Hormuz, with a response from Donald Trump expected by Sunday.

media:group.media:description: US President Donald Trump speaks to reporters over the North Atlantic as he returns to Washington from the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, aboard Air Force One, US, January 22, 2026.

item.title: 'Midwits' and media bust-ups herald One Nation's arrival in SA parliament
item.description: One Nation broke its parliamentary silence to talk about culture wars and political barneys. But not everything went as expected.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Kelsall

media:group.media:description: One Nation state leader Cory Bernardi and federal leader Pauline Hanson the opening of South Australia's new parliamentary term earlier this month.

item.title: 'Unusual' Greek island listed for half the price of average Aussie home
item.description: Makri Island, not far from mainland Greece, boasts almost 100 hectares of uninhabited Mediterranean beauty, but buyers be warned, this slice of paradise comes with a catch.
item.dc:creator: Riley Stuart

media:group.media:description: Makri Island is a slice of paradise with plenty of problems.

item.title: Labor's tax defence is backfiring with young voters
item.description: Labor is banking on younger generations, who are overwhelmingly wage earners, being more interested in owning their own home than pocketing higher profits on a lucky crypto trade.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Transcripts of press conferences and interviews with Jim Chalmers and Anthony Albanese this week are littered with accusations "political opponents" and their "allies" are distorting Labor's budget to suit their own ends.

item.title: As a kid, Thitiwoot wanted to name a dino. He never knew it'd be this big
item.description: It started out as a bone sticking out of the mud next to a lake in Thailand — and now scientists say it's the biggest dinosaur ever found in South-East Asia.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Webb

media:group.media:description: The Nagatitan's humerus or upper front leg bone, seen here in replica alongside Mr Sethapanichsakul, was 1.78m tall.

item.title: Rachel Ward gave up glamour for cows and a not-so-simple country life
item.description: With the country wind in her grey hair, cow dung on her boots and not a skerrick of make-up, former actor Rachel Ward has found her place, sharing her passion for regenerative farming.
item.dc:creator: Leisa Scott and Vanessa Gorman

media:group.media:description: Rachel Ward with her Andy Warhol painting.

item.title: Trump revives centuries-old doctrine that shaped US-Cuba relations
item.description: The Monroe Doctrine was once a core tenet of US foreign policy. US President Donald Trump has brought it back with a new interpretation.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Steedman

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump has revived the Monroe Doctrine a decade after the Obama administration said the policy was "over".

item.title: The scale of the mouse plague is hard to fathom. Here's a visualisation
item.description: A mouse plague is invading farms and homes in southern and Western Australia. But what does that really look like?
item.dc:creator: Ashley Kyd, Jarrod Fankhauser, Andie Noonan, Jessica Riga, Matt Liddy, Joanna Prendergast, and Chris Lewis

item.title: Economists welcome budget and reply, even if they don't like every policy
item.description: Five leading economists weigh in on the budget and reply debate, embracing many of the ideas raised while also arguing that "bigger tax reform is needed."
item.dc:creator: Gareth Hutchens

media:group.media:description: Jim Chalmers has been praised by economists for presenting a bold tax reform program, even if they don't agree with every element of it.

item.title: 'Not a fringe hobby for inner-city weirdos': What's blocking bikes in cities?
item.description: Climate change, the cost-of-living crisis and healthy lifestyle goals should make active transport like cycling a no brainer. So why does Australia struggle to build a bike culture?
item.dc:creator: Rhiannon Stevens

item.title: Meet the women turning bicycle tyres and timber offcuts into furniture
item.description: These artists are carving out a niche in a traditionally male space, using recycled materials and offcuts to create beautiful furniture, and even making music to accompany the work.
item.dc:creator: Jane Rocca

media:group.media:description: Jess Humpston uses her background in fashion and interior design to inform her woodworking.

item.title: Jamie-Lee Price thinks fake tan could solve a lot of netball's problems
item.description: Frustrated at netball's stagnation, this is what Jamie-Lee Price wants to see happen to keep her sport moving with the times.
item.dc:creator: Marnie Vinall

media:group.media:description: Jamie-Lee Price got engaged last month.

item.title: Put away the dictionary. It's spelling bee time!
item.description: Know your way around a homonym? Nah, me neither but come in and have a crack at our Sunday quiz anyway.

media:group.media:description: Guy Montgomery on the set of Guy Mont-Spelling Bee for season three.

item.title: Auckland makes A-League history with grand-final triumph over Sydney
item.description: Auckland has become the first side from New Zealand to win an A-League Men championship after downing competition heavyweight Sydney 1-0 in a cagey season decider.

media:group.media:description: Auckland claimed a history making championship against Sydney.

item.title: PM defends negative gearing, capital gains tax changes at Labor conference
item.description: The prime minister became emotional at one point as he spoke at a Labor conference in Melbourne to defend the budget changes.
item.dc:creator: Evelyn Manfield

media:group.media:description: Anthony Albanese spoke at the Victorian Labor Party’s state conference in Melbourne.

item.title: One Nation set to become federal opposition, poll forecasts
item.description: One Nation would win dozens of seats if a federal election was held today, according to a major opinion poll.
item.dc:creator: Evelyn Manfield

media:group.media:description: A major opinion poll predicts One Nation would win dozens of seats if a federal election was held today.

item.title: 'Stay with us': Dragons booed off the field at home ahead of fan day
item.description: St George Illawarra is 0-11 to start the 2026 season after losing to the Warriors in Kogarah, while the Sea Eagles continue their strong form with a hard-fought victory over Gold Coast.
item.dc:creator: Nick Campton and Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: The Dragons are on the road for their next four games.

item.title: Caution and long-term thinking as budget adds to softening real estate market
item.description: After riding a post-COVID wave of booming prices, real estate agents say SA's housing market is starting to level out, as federal budget tax changes and rising interest rates reduce buyer appetite.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Richards, Eugene Boisvert, and Peta Doherty

media:group.media:description: Sam Doman says investors will be focused on the long-term.

item.title: Death toll reaches 82 after Chinese coal mine blast
item.description: A gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China's Shanxi province sits at 82, state media reports. Two people remain missing.

media:group.media:description: Scores of people have been reported dead following a blast at a coal mine in China.

item.title: Charges withdrawn against artist for displaying Nazi symbols in artwork
item.description: The case has been dropped against an Australian artist charged with displaying Nazi symbols on an artwork depicting politicians and billionaires.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Clifford

media:group.media:description: Charges against Wagga designer Michael Agzarian have been dropped.

item.title: 'Beautiful, kind, caring man' dies in hospital months after alleged bashing
item.description: Friends of Darwin man Martin Wiberg, 48, are remembering him as a "very gentle soul" after he died in hospital this week.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Barrett Peters

media:group.media:description: Martin Wiberg is being remembered as an independent thinker.

item.title: Queensland jockey dies in hospital after training accident
item.description: Both of Shane McGovern's legs were amputated during a weeks-long stay in hospital.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Chomicki and Olivia Nunes-Malek

media:group.media:description: Shane McGovern is being mourned after his death at 67.

item.title: Brent Harvey is about to lose his place in AFL history
item.description: Brent 'Boomer' Harvey has enjoyed being the AFL/VFL games record holder for the past decade, and says he could have played more if he'd accepted an offer from other clubs to extend his career.
item.dc:creator: Alexia Pesce

media:group.media:description: Brent Harvey says he knocked back offers from other clubs to continue his career.

item.title: 'There's more in the tank:' Pendlebury celebrates games record despite Eagles scare
item.description: Collingwood's No.10 enjoys a 10-point win in his record-breaking match as the Magpies hold firm to down the Eagles at the MCG.
item.dc:creator: Dean Bilton and Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: Pendlebury enjoyed a 10-point win on his record-breaking game.
